title: Trump Squeezes Immigrants by Cutting Them Off From Jobs, Health Care and Housing
summary: The methodically planned strategy is intended to pressure noncitizens, including many with legal status, to leave the United States.

title: Judge Reopens Trump’s Lawsuit Demanding $10 Billion From IRS
summary: The ruling was a blow to both President Trump, who had voluntarily dismissed the suit last week, and to the Justice Department, which used the suit to establish a fund likely intended for Trump allies.

summary: A federal judge ordered the Kennedy Center to take President Trump’s name off the building. What happens next?

title: Inside the Ebola Epicenter, the Virus Rages With Little to Stop It
summary: A remote gold mining town is under siege, as medical workers struggle to beat back a surge of deaths and infections.

title: What to Know About the Ebola Outbreak
summary: Aid agencies are racing to help underequipped health workers in the Democratic Republic of Congo. More than 245 people are now suspected to have died from the virus.

summary: Tom Steyer wants to address California’s housing crisis if he becomes governor. He is also a billionaire with extensive personal real estate holdings.

title: A Candidate’s Past Ties to a Militant Cleric Are Surfacing in a N.J. House Race
summary: Dr. Adam Hamawy, a New Jersey Democrat with big-name support on the left, has touted his humanitarian work, but some opponents want to talk about Sheikh Omar Abdel Rahman.

title: A Clean Energy PAC Helped Beat Chip Roy, and Now It Has New Targets
summary: A PAC representing wind and solar energy interests spent $1.1 million to boost the Republican primary opponent of Chip Roy, an opponent of renewables. Now they are trying to save a Republican ally in Iowa.

summary: The president has wavered on whether to move ahead with an agreement with Iran to end the war. On Friday, he vowed to make a “final determination” soon.

summary: From Iran to Gaza, Eid al-Adha celebrations were muted as war dragged on and shortages of food and fuel roiled the region.

summary: The failure to protect NATO territory further increased anxiety about alliance solidarity, Russia’s intentions and Washington’s commitment to collective defense.

summary: When a drone crashed into an apartment building in eastern Romania, residents were reminded that the Russia-Ukraine war makes for a dangerous neighbor.

title: How Ukraine’s Street Style Is Shaped by the War
summary: In Kyiv, military-inspired fashion can be seen everywhere. Soldiers and civilians alike say it’s a show of solidarity, not a fad.

summary: Tennis is a solitary sport, but with new fertility benefits, a sisterhood of egg freezers is emerging in locker rooms and players’ lounges.

summary: I once raised pigs. And factory farms raise them today in conditions that are as unconscionable as they are invisible.

summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has not filled the vacant role of president of the Economic Development Corporation, deepening concern over his attention to the New York City economy.

summary: Colombia’s leftist candidate for president leads in polls, but his main rival — a far-right outsider who calls himself “The Tiger” — has gained momentum. Elections are Sunday.

summary: West Virginia is home to the country’s last greyhound races, a sport that once drew millions of spectators each year but may soon be banned nationwide.
